# My Mechanic QLD

> Professional mobile mechanic providing on-site car servicing and repairs across Brisbane, Logan, Ipswich and the Gold Coast in South East Queensland, Australia. Qualified mechanics with 15+ years of dealership experience. Fixed-price quotes given in writing before any work begins. Every repair backed by a workmanship warranty, alongside customer rights under the Australian Consumer Law.

My Mechanic QLD is a professional mobile mechanic business based in Springwood, Queensland. The team holds Cert III in Light Vehicle Mechanical Technology and has 15+ years inside dealership workshops in South East Queensland. The business provides mobile mechanical services at the customer's home, office or kerbside, eliminating the time customers typically lose to a workshop service. The service van carries professional-grade diagnostic tools, the common consumables, and the specific parts for the booked job.

The four core services are brake repair, alternator and starter motor replacement, radiator and water pump replacement, and logbook servicing. Additional services include pre-purchase inspections, battery replacement, warning-light diagnostics, suspension repair, and emergency call-outs. The business does not offer air-conditioning repairs, auto electrical work beyond batteries and code reading, panel beating, tyre fitting, wheel alignments, or work on trucks, trailers, machinery or motorcycles.

Pricing is fixed and quoted in writing before any spanners come out. Under the Australian Consumer Law, having logbook servicing performed by a qualified mobile mechanic does not void a vehicle's new-car warranty, provided manufacturer specifications are followed.

## Business details
- Phone: +61 451 159 954 (Australian: 0451 159 954)
- Email: contact@mymechanicqld.com.au
- Trading hours: Monday to Friday 7am to 6pm, Saturday 8am to 5pm, closed Sundays
- Based: Springwood, Queensland 4127, Australia
- Service area: Brisbane, Logan, Ipswich and the Gold Coast


## Services
- [Brake repair](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/brake-repairs/): Mobile brake pad and rotor replacement, hydraulic diagnosis, and fluid flush. OEM or quality aftermarket parts, sized for the vehicle. Fixed-price quote upfront, workmanship warranty per our terms.
- [Alternator and starter motor replacement](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/starter-alternator/): On-site charging-system diagnosis (battery, alternator, starter) for cars that won't start, slow-crank, or stall under load. Fixed-price quote upfront, workmanship warranty per our terms.
- [Radiator and water pump replacement](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/radiator-cooling-system/): Cooling-system pressure test and repair for overheating, coolant leaks, sweet-smelling cabin, or temperature gauge climbing. Fixed-price quote upfront, workmanship warranty per our terms.
- [Logbook and general servicing](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/logbook-servicing/): Manufacturer-spec scheduled servicing with OEM or quality aftermarket filters and correct-grade oils. Warranty-safe under Australian Consumer Law. Logbook stamped, invoice emailed.
- [Pre-purchase inspection](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/pre-purchase-inspection/): Independent comprehensive inspection at the seller's address before buying a used car. Includes road test and written report with photos.
- [Battery replacement](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/battery-replacement/): Mobile heavy-duty battery replacement, delivered and fitted on-site. Quality replacement batteries with manufacturer warranties up to 24 to 36 months. Old battery recycled.
- [Warning-light diagnostics](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/car-diagnostics/): Professional OBD scan plus physical fault-finding for check-engine, ABS, transmission, traction-control and airbag warning lights.
- [Steering and suspension repair](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/steering-suspension/): Mobile replacement of ball joints, control arms, bushes, tie-rod ends, shocks and struts. OEM or quality aftermarket parts.

## Pricing and warranty
- [How our pricing works](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/pricing/): How we quote, what is included, and how to get a real number for your specific vehicle.
- [Workmanship and parts warranty](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/warranty/): Detailed explanation of what is covered, what is not, how to claim, and how it sits alongside statutory rights under the Australian Consumer Law.
- [How a mobile mechanic works](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/how-it-works/): Six-step process from first phone call to keys back in customer's hand.

## Educational content (frequently cited topics)
- [Does a mobile logbook service void your new-car warranty?](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/does-a-mobile-logbook-service-void-your-new-car-warranty/): No. The Australian Consumer Law protects the customer's right to use any qualified mechanic, including a mobile one, as long as manufacturer specifications are followed and appropriate parts are used. The dealership cannot refuse warranty for this reason.
- [How much does a brake job cost in Brisbane?](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/how-much-does-a-brake-job-cost-in-brisbane/): What goes into a brake repair quote and why prices vary by vehicle. Fixed-price quoted upfront for your specific car.
- [How often should you service your car?](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/how-often-should-you-service-your-car/): Most modern Australian cars need a service every 10,000 to 15,000 km or every 6 to 12 months, whichever comes first. Short-trip drivers should halve the interval.
- [Won't start? How to tell if it's the battery, alternator or starter](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/car-wont-start-battery-alternator-or-starter/): Quick diagnostic: dim dashboard lights mean battery, single click with bright dashboard means starter, car starts but goes flat next morning means alternator.
- [Why is my car overheating?](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/why-is-my-car-overheating/): Seven common causes ranked by frequency, including low coolant from a slow leak, failed thermostat, failing water pump, blocked radiator, non-working cooling fan, blown head gasket.
- [Check engine light: what does it actually mean?](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/check-engine-light-what-does-it-mean/): Steady light is usually non-urgent; flashing light means active misfire that can destroy the catalytic converter within hundreds of kilometres.
- [Signs your brakes need replacing](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/signs-your-brakes-need-replacing/): Six warning signs explained: high-pitched squeal (pad wear indicator), grinding (pads worn through), pedal pulsing (warped rotors), soft pedal (hydraulic issue), pulling under brakes, dashboard warning light.
- [Brake pads vs rotors: which one is the noise?](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/brake-pads-vs-rotors-which-one-is-the-noise/): Squealing is pads, grinding is pads damaging rotors, pulsing is rotors warped, soft pedal is hydraulics.
- [Pre-purchase inspection checklist](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/pre-purchase-inspection-checklist/): Full checklist organized by under-bonnet, underneath, body and interior, and on-road testing.
- [Mobile mechanic vs workshop: when each one wins](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/blog/mobile-mechanic-vs-workshop-when-each-one-wins/): Honest comparison. Mobile wins for routine servicing, brakes, batteries, alternators, water pumps, pre-purchase inspections, diagnostics. Workshops genuinely better for wheel alignments, engine-out work, panel beating, transmission rebuilds, tyre fitting.

## Service areas
- [Service area overview](https://www.mymechanicqld.com.au/areas/): Suburbs across Brisbane, Logan, Ipswich and the northern Gold Coast.
- Brisbane suburbs covered: Sunnybank, Mt Gravatt, Carindale, Wynnum, Cleveland, Chermside, Aspley, Sandgate, Indooroopilly, Toowong, Nundah, Kelvin Grove, Coorparoo, Bulimba, Forest Lake.
- Logan suburbs covered: Springwood (home base), Beenleigh, Browns Plains, Loganholme.
- Ipswich suburbs covered: Springfield, Goodna, Redbank Plains, Brassall.
- Gold Coast suburbs covered: Coomera, Helensvale, Nerang, Southport, Robina.
